portfolio_view: Four picks spanning Oil & Gas (PSU refining), Healthcare (specialty pharma/biosimilars), Capital Goods (EMS/electronics manufacturing), and Financial Services (wealth management) — deliberately no sector overlap, and three of the four (CHENNPETRO, SHILPAMED, SYRMA) are independently confirmed by this week's daily-analyst breakout layer, a strong convergence signal. HFCL was dropped despite its #2 mechanical rank and highest RS (100) in the pool: deep research found the loss-to-profit turnaround is real, but valuation (P/E ~135-387x) and a reported 41-analyst "Strong Sell" consensus directly contradict the momentum read — a case where research should override the mechanical score. LAURUSLABS (daily-analyst BUY) was passed over as the weaker, lower-conviction healthcare name once SHILPAMED already filled that slot.

meta.highlights: smart-money 100: FII 8.8->14.98%; DII 2.6->1.01%; delivery %/bulk deals pending (Phase C); balance-sheet 91: deleveraging: debt 4260 -> 1964 Cr; earnings 91: qtr PAT YoY +200%; accelerating (TTM 1349% > 3y -4%); governance 85: no pledge disclosed; auditor/SEBI/related-party checks pending (Phase C)

picks.selected_because: Highest conviction in the pool (72.5), RS 93, PSU refining turnaround confirmed by the daily-analyst layer (HIGH, FULL PLAN, 09 Jul).
picks.thesis: GRM expansion is real, not inventory gains — FY26 avg GRM $9.28/bbl vs $4.22 FY25, Q4 spiked to $13.75/bbl, 112% capacity utilization, debt cut 4260→1964 Cr. Cheapest refiner on the board (~6-7x P/E vs BPCL/HPCL/MRPL), with IOC-backed Cauvery Basin Refinery JV adding future capacity.
picks.catalyst: Q1 FY27 results due at the board meeting on 23 Jul 2026 — five days out.
picks.management: IOC + NICO promoter stake ~67%, PSU/CAG-audited, no governance flags found, dividends robust.
picks.risks: Refining margins are structurally cyclical — Brent swinging $72-88/bbl with IEA flagging 3.7-4.0mbpd 2026 oversupply risk (Goldman/JPMorgan bearish crude calls); capex ramp to ₹700-800cr/yr raises D/E to ~2.33x for the new JV.
picks.watch_for: GRM compression or a Q1 FY27 miss on 23 Jul.

meta.highlights: earnings 100: qtr PAT YoY +200%; accelerating (TTM 165% > 3y 109%); technicals/RS 98: RS percentile 97; TT 8/8, stage 2, VCP live; valuation 95: P/E 52 cheaper than growth (PEG 0.48); smart-money 64: FII 10.92->11.39%; DII 7.67->8.05%; delivery %/bulk deals pending (Phase C)

picks.selected_because: Conviction 70.1, RS 97, cheapest-for-growth valuation (PEG 0.48), confirmed by daily-analyst BUY (MEDIUM/HALF PLAN, 08 Jul).
picks.thesis: Margin-driven turnaround verified — Q4 FY26 gross margin jumped 12.6%→30.4%, PAT +643% YoY. Real pipeline momentum: Unicycive's OLC FDA approval activated Shilpa's Raichur commercial block, plus new Orion nivolumab-biosimilar and SteinCares LatAm deals. Chinese API oversupply is a genuine input-cost tailwind.
picks.catalyst: Raichur block ramp and near-term biosimilar licensing deliveries.
picks.management: Bhutada family, ~49-50% holding, no pledge; a May-26 USFDA Form 483 (Bengaluru Unit VI) was procedural-only, notably cleaner than peers hit with warning letters.
picks.risks: the exact "+200%" mechanical PAT figure doesn't match any single reported print (closest verified: Q4 +643%, FY26 full-year ~+211%) — directionally right, not literal; USFDA follow-through risk remains open.
picks.watch_for: outcome of USFDA re-inspection following the Form 483.

meta.highlights: smart-money 100: FII 6.34->6.6%; DII 9.15->16.59%; delivery %/bulk deals pending (Phase C); earnings 92: qtr PAT YoY +68%; accelerating (TTM 102% > 3y 39%); technicals/RS 84: RS percentile 98; TT 8/8, stage 2, VCP none; balance-sheet 72: clean balance sheet

picks.selected_because: Conviction 69.1, RS 98, DII buying sharply (9.15%→16.59%), confirmed by daily-analyst BUY (MEDIUM/HALF PLAN, 08 Jul) and named HDFC Securities' EMS "top pick."
picks.thesis: Broad-based, verified growth (Q4 FY26 PAT +67%, FY26 +87.5%) across consumer/auto/healthcare/industrial; ₹6,600cr order book. New ₹1,800cr PCB/copper-clad-laminate JV with Korea's Shinhyup moves Syrma upstream into materials, riding the China+1 sourcing shift.
picks.catalyst: PCB/CCL JV trial production targeted Dec-2026.
picks.management: Tandon/Gujral promoter group (~42%), no red flags; held up notably better than EMS peers (Kaynes, Dixon, Amber) during the Nov'25-Feb'26 sector selloff.
picks.risks: rich valuation (P/E ~93x, P/B ~9.2x); customer concentration (top 20 = 63% of revenue) and low FCF conversion (~28%).
picks.watch_for: FY27 margin guidance (10.5-11%) undershooting the 12% just delivered.

meta.highlights: earnings 94: qtr PAT YoY +73%; accelerating (TTM 45% > 3y 33%); smart-money 91: FII 5.56->6.37%; DII 8.73->10.39%; delivery %/bulk deals pending (Phase C); governance 85: no pledge disclosed; auditor/SEBI/related-party checks pending (Phase C); technicals/RS 82: RS percentile 94; TT 8/8, stage 2, VCP none

picks.selected_because: Conviction 68.1, RS 94, Quality archetype — the only financial-services diversifier in the set, with confirmed accelerating earnings.
picks.thesis: AUM crossed ₹1 lakh crore (+21% YoY), UHNI mix nearly doubled to 30.1% share, Q1 FY27 PAT +73.8% YoY already delivering 25% of full-year guidance in one quarter. Structural tailwind: Indian wealth-management TAM projected to nearly double to $2.3T by FY29.
picks.catalyst: Quarterly delivery against FY27 guidance of ₹460cr PAT.
picks.management: Client attrition down to just 0.09% of AUM — a sticky franchise signal.
picks.risks: research surfaced a DISCREPANCY with the mechanical "no pledge" read — 2026 filings show ~5.3% pledge at the Anand Rathi group level (one filing was later called "inadvertent" and corrected) — verify before sizing; also market-linked revenue and rich valuation (P/E ~74-83x vs 360 One ~35x).
picks.watch_for: confirmation of the promoter-pledge status — if a genuine, standing pledge is confirmed, this should be re-vetoed.
